description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden cabinet featuring rounded edges and a polished finish. The front panel displays a large circular analog tuning dial with frequency markings and a central decorative emblem, flanked by three horizontal black speaker grilles on the left. Below the dial are four black rotary knobs, likely for tuning, volume, and other controls. The sides and back are plain, showcasing the wood grain, while the top is smooth and unadorned. The overall design is reminiscent of radios from the early to mid-20th century, combining both functional and decorative elements. The radio's construction suggests a hollow interior housing electronic components and a speaker.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a large circular tuning dial, black speaker grilles, and four rotary knobs on the front. The cabinet has a polished wood finish and rounded edges.
description_view_0: Front view showing the analog tuning dial, three black speaker grilles, and four rotary knobs.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the smooth, polished wooden surface with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain, dark wooden back panel with no visible openings or controls.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, featuring a smooth wooden surface without any controls.
